title: Ex vivo mouse kidney slice experiment
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"an9t1","text":"This protocol was used in a manuscript \"Sodium-calcium exchanger 1 is the key molecule for urinary potassium excretion against hyperkalemia.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"cf5f","text":"Hank's Balanced salt Solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":29}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3npb7","text":"110 mM NaCl ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"crrbi","text":"    3 mM KCl","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f7gvb","text":"1.2 mM MgSO","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1g852","text":"1.8 mM CaCl2","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":11,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aq8qc","text":"   4 mM Na acetate","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6o3cj","text":"   1 mM Na citrate","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8i3fm","text":"   6 mM D-glucose","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"362p7","text":"   6 mM L-alanine ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"28q6t","text":"   1 mM NaH2PO4","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":11,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":14,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c8c21","text":"   3 mM Na2HPO4 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":10,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":14,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9igt4","text":" 25 mM NaHCO3","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":12,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Make an incision then cut the skin from the bottom to the top of the mice followed the abdominal line with the round side of the scissors.
Cut the lateral skin to give free access to the abdomen.
With your finger, displace the intestine to the right, who should see the kidney.
Clip the sternum with the curve forceps, cut the ribs, do not cut the lungs, give access to the heart by moving the forceps. The heart should beat.
Pick the needle (22 gauge) through the right ventricle.
Cut the right auriula and perfused at 17 ml/min with Hank's balanced salt solution (HBSS) pH 7.4 at 37°C equilibrated with 5%CO 2 /95%O 2
Perfuse, until the kidneys are clear of blood, the kidneys appear green-white.
Immediately, they were removed. Take out the capsule, cut the kidney in pieces of 3 to 5 mm thick.

Put these pieces in bubbling buffer at room temperature. Start immediately to cut thinner slices (approximately 0.5 mm) using a micro slicer (Natume Seisakusho CO., Ltd, Tokyo, Japan). Cut from the cortex to the papilla in order to avoid kidney damages.
The slices were incubated at room temperature for 20 min in equilibrated HBSS for recovery before treatment. The tissue slice should always be in bubbling solution.
Hold the tube in the 20 ml scintillation vial . The vial should contain 10 ml of drug solution (3 ml is the minimum).
The slices will be treated with drugs. Treatment should be in bubbling solution at room temperature.
After treatment, the slices were snap frozen in liquid nitrogen.
creator: Wakana Shoda
